---
title: "Foster Students and Students Experiencing Homelessness"
format: 
  html:
    theme: zephyr
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#| echo = FALSE
#| warning = FALSE
#| message = FALSE 
#| fig.width = 10
#| fig.height = 6)

library(tidyverse)
library(DT)
library(wesanderson)
library(png)
library(ggtext)
library(ggdist)
library(systemfonts)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Register font for weepeople
register_font(
  name = "weepeople",
  plain = "www/weepeople.ttf"
)

wee_letters <- c("Q", "b", "V", "h")

# Load data
dashboard <- read_csv("data/ca_dashboard.csv")
enrollment <- read_csv("data/enrollment.csv")
homeless <- read_csv("data/homeless.csv") %>% 
  filter(charter_school == "All" | aggregate_level == "S", # schools (charter == Yes | No)
         dass == "All" | aggregate_level == "S") %>%       # or All schools at higher aggregate levels
  mutate(percent_homeless = homeless_student_enrollment/cumulative_enrollment * 100,
         across(ends_with("(percent)"), parse_number))

dashboard <- 
  dashboard %>% 
  mutate(statuslevel = as_factor(statuslevel),
         reportingyear = fct_relevel(as_factor(reportingyear), "2019"),
         indicator = fct_relevel(as_factor(indicator), 
                                 "ELA", "Math", "ELPI", 
                                 "absenteeism", "graduation", 
                                 "suspension", "college/career"),
         studentgroup = as_factor(studentgroup),
         student_group_long = fct_relevel(as_factor(student_group_long),
                                          "All students",
                                          "Black/African American",
                                          "American Indian or Alaska Native",
                                          "Asian",
                                          "Filipino",
                                          "Hispanic",
                                          "Pacific Islander",
                                          "White",
                                          "Multiple Races/Two or more",
                                          "Socioeconomically Disadvantaged",
                                          "Homeless Youth",
                                          "Students with Disabilities",
                                          "Foster Youth",
                                          "English Learner",
                                          "English Learners Only",
                                          "RFEPs Only",
                                          "English Only",
                                          "Smarter Balanced Assessment",
                                          "CA Alternative Assessment"
         ),
         priority_eligible = as_factor(priority_eligible),
         indicator_eligible = as_factor(indicator_eligible),
         charter_flag = if_else(is.na(charter_flag), "N", charter_flag),
         color = as_factor(color)
  )

homeless_to_orange <- read_csv("data/homeless_to_orange.csv")

years <- 
  dashboard %>% 
  select(reportingyear) %>% 
  distinct()

counties <- 
  homeless %>% 
  select(county_name) %>% 
  distinct()

districts <- 
  homeless %>%
  select(county_name, district_name) %>% 
  distinct()

schools <- 
  homeless %>% 
  select(county_name, district_name, school_name) %>% 
  distinct()

categories_for_districts <- 
  homeless %>%
  filter(aggregate_level == "D") %>% 
  select(reporting_group) %>% 
  distinct()

student_groups <-
  dashboard %>% 
  select(studentgroup, student_group_long) %>% 
  distinct()

latest_school_year = "2023-24"

status_colors <- c(
  "lightgrey", "tomato", "orange",
  "yellow", "mediumseagreen", "dodgerblue", "darkslategrey"
)

# Parameters that would have been inputs in Shiny
# Default values
selected_county <- "Solano"
district_options <- districts %>% filter(county_name == selected_county)
county_office <- district_options %>% 
  filter(str_detect(district_name, "Office")) %>% 
  pull(district_name) %>% 
  nth(1)

selected_district <- county_office
school_options <- schools %>% 
  filter(county_name == selected_county,
         district_name == selected_district) %>% 
  distinct()
selected_school <- school_options$school_name[1]
selected_group <- "Race/Ethnicity"

# Prepare data based on selections
homeless_in_district <- homeless %>%
  filter(county_name == selected_county,
         district_name == selected_district)

homeless_in_school <- homeless_in_district %>% 
  filter(school_name == selected_school)

selected_subgroups <- homeless_in_district %>% 
  filter(reporting_group == selected_group,
         academic_year == latest_school_year,
         aggregate_level == "D")

homeless_in_school_this_year <- homeless_in_district %>% 
  filter(school_name == selected_school,
         reporting_category == "TA",
         academic_year == latest_school_year) %>% 
  pull(homeless_student_enrollment) %>% 
  nth(1)
```

```{r homeless_table}
# Create a table showing homeless student counts by reporting group
selected_subgroups %>%
  select(reporting_long, homeless_student_enrollment) %>%
  arrange(desc(homeless_student_enrollment)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(homeless_student_enrollment), homeless_student_enrollment > 0) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Group", "Number of Homeless Students"),
        caption = paste("Students Experiencing Homelessness by", selected_group)) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))

```

```{r homeless_groups}

selected_subgroups %>%
  filter(!is.na(homeless_student_enrollment), homeless_student_enrollment > 0) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(mapping = aes(y = reorder(reporting_long, homeless_student_enrollment),
                         x = homeless_student_enrollment,
                         fill = reporting_long)) +
  labs(title = paste("Students in", selected_district,
                    "Experiencing Homelessness by", selected_group),
       y = NULL,
       x = "Number of Students",
       fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```
```{r homeless_icons}

cat("### Students Experiencing Homelessness in", selected_district, "-", selected_school, "\n")
cat("**Total number of students who experienced housing insecurity in", selected_school, latest_school_year, ":**", homeless_in_school_this_year, "\n")
```


```{r homeless_trends}

homeless_in_school %>% 
  filter(reporting_category == "TA") %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = as_factor(academic_year), 
                          y = homeless_student_enrollment,
                          group = school_name),
            linewidth = 2,
            color = "skyblue") +
  expand_limits(y = 0) +
  labs(title = paste("Students Experiencing Homelessness in\n", 
                     selected_district,
                     selected_school,
                     "\nsince", homeless_in_school$academic_year[1]),
       x = "Academic Year",
       y = "Number of Students") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

```

```{r homeless_status}

# Create a table for housing status with count and percent columns
homeless_in_school %>% 
  filter(reporting_category == "TA", academic_year == latest_school_year) %>%
  # First, select and rename the columns to make them easier to work with
  select(
    doubled_up_count = temporarily_doubled_up,
    doubled_up_percent = `temporarily_doubled_up_(percent)`,
    shelters_count = temporary_shelters,
    shelters_percent = `temporary_shelters_(percent)`,
    hotels_count = `hotels/motels`,
    hotels_percent = `hotels/motels_(percent)`,
    unsheltered_count = temporarily_unsheltered,
    unsheltered_percent = `temporarily_unsheltered_(percent)`,
    unknown_count = `missing/unknown`,
    unknown_percent = `missing/unknown_(percent)`
  ) %>%
  # Reshape the data to a longer format for easier processing
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = c("housing_status", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.+)_(count|percent)"
  ) %>%
  # Improve the display names and format values
  mutate(
    housing_status = case_when(
      housing_status == "doubled_up" ~ "Temporarily Doubled Up",
      housing_status == "shelters" ~ "Temporary Shelters",
      housing_status == "hotels" ~ "Hotels/Motels",
      housing_status == "unsheltered" ~ "Temporarily Unsheltered",
      housing_status == "unknown" ~ "Missing/Unknown",
      TRUE ~ housing_status
    ),
    # Format the percent with % symbol
    percent = paste0(round(percent, 1), "%"),
    # Format the count as integer
    count = format(count, big.mark = ",")
  ) %>%
  # Sort by count in descending order
  arrange(desc(as.numeric(gsub(",", "", count)))) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Housing Status", "Count", "Percent"),
    caption = paste("Housing Status for Homeless Students in", selected_school),
    align = c("l", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))

```
